name: Talatí de Dalt
description: One of the most picturesque and accessible Talayotic sites, part of the UNESCO listing. Its taula, with a leaning column resting against it and ringed by centuries-old olive trees, is among the most recognisable images of Menorcan prehistory.
imageAlt: The taula of Talatí de Dalt with its fallen pillar leaning against it, among olive trees at the Talayotic settlement in Menorca.

highlights: the taula with its leaning supporting pillar, surrounded by olive trees
step into the caves and nooks of the talayotic settlement (UNESCO)
go first or last thing: there is little shade
